rubric: Independent human ground truth, authored from the transcript ALONE before running any extractor (regex, Haiku, or Opus). Inclusion rule: a DECISION is any statement by the chair or a named participant that records a settled outcome, recognisable by a governing verb (approved / agreed to adopt / rejected / deferred / will require / noted) applied to a concrete spectrum-policy thing. An ACTION is a future commitment of the form '<owner> will <do thing>' or '<owner> to <do thing>'. An open QUESTION is an unresolved interrogative raised for the record. Items deliberately span BOTH the labelled-prefix form (DECISION:/ACTION:/QUESTION:, which the regex baseline can catch) and the unlabelled conversational form (which the regex baseline structurally cannot catch) so the regex->Haiku gap is measurable. This file is the measurement reference; it is NOT derived from any extractor's output.

decisions.text: Approved the 3.45 GHz commercial sharing framework for Phase 2.
The working group agreed to adopt the dynamic protection contour methodology.
The committee rejected the fixed exclusion-zone proposal for coastal radars.
Deferred the 7 GHz incumbent transition timeline to the next quarterly review.
We will require all SAS operators to file revised interference budgets.
Noted the NTIA objection to the proposed aggregate EIRP limit.

actions.text: Carol will circulate the revised interference budget template by next Friday.
Dan to coordinate the field-measurement campaign with the Navy testing office.
Priya compiles the public comment summary before the May 30 deadline.
The secretariat will publish the updated coordination procedures on the docket.

questions.text: Do we need a separate eval track for non-federal incumbents in the 7 GHz band?
How should the aggregate EIRP limit be enforced across overlapping SAS territories?
Should the Phase 2 framework include a sunset clause?
